docs.title: Dance Central Definitive Documentation
docs.text:
This documentation covers the full build and content workflow for this repository.

If you only want to install and play the game, start with Play the Game.

Use it as the source of truth for:

  • preparing your environment,
  • building patch output,
  • managing source data changes,
  • testing in Xenia,
  • troubleshooting common issues.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
The project is centered around converting and packaging game data from src/ into a deployable output tree in out/.

The build system supports:

  • multiple XEX variants (deluxe/vanilla, retail/debug),
  • platform-specific tool binaries (Linux/macOS/Windows),
  • optional include overlays,
  • optional Xenia patch generation and launch.

docs.title: Read this first
docs.text:
Start with Getting Started, then continue to Build System.

If you are changing gameplay data or assets, review Modding Workflow.

If something fails, check Troubleshooting.

For licensing and IP scope, read Legal and Licensing.

docs.title: Core pipeline
docs.text:
The build pipeline is orchestrated by tools/build.py and powered by Ninja.

High-level flow:

  1. Clean/create working directories.
  2. Copy filtered source data from src/ to obj/src/.
  3. Generate build rules into build.ninja.
  4. Convert/copy files into obj/ark/ and obj/raw/.
  5. Pack output ark/hdr into out/gen/.
  6. Copy selected XEX binaries into out/.
  7. Optionally generate Xenia patch and run Xenia.

docs.title: File conversion behavior
docs.text:
• .png -> converted with superfreq to .png_xbox
  • .dta -> validated with dtacheck, serialized and encrypted via dtab to .dtb
  • Other files -> copied as-is

Platform-specific tools are selected from:

  • tools/linux/
  • tools/macos/
  • tools/windows/

docs.title: Build variants
docs.text:
By default, the build includes deluxe retail.

Flags add more variants:

  • --vanilla adds vanilla retail
  • --debug adds debug builds for whichever families are enabled

docs.title: CLI reference
docs.text:
./tools/build.py src bin [options]

Common options:

  • --output <dir> set output root
  • --includes <dir> include additional files in output
  • --clean remove generated cache directories after successful build
  • --allow-dtacheck-errors keep building even if dtacheck reports errors
  • --patch-output <dir> copy Xenia patch to directory
  • --xenia-root <path> Xenia root directory (patch will be copied to patches/ subdirectory)
  • --xenia-path <path> path to Xenia executable
  • --xenia-run 0..4 auto-launch selected build in Xenia
  • --xenia-args "..." pass extra args to Xenia

docs.title: Wrapper scripts
docs.text:
• scripts/build.sh: Linux/macOS wrapper for default build
  • scripts/build.bat: Windows wrapper for default build

Both wrappers call tools/build.py with src bin.

docs.title: Prebuilt release ZIP
docs.text:
1. Download the prebuilt ZIP from the GitHub release.
  2. Extract it into your game folder, the one that already contains default.xex and gen/.
  3. Launch definitive.xex.

Optional: Back up default.xex and rename definitive.xex to default.xex if you want the base game hidden from the dashboard.

The ZIP also includes the Xenia patch under xenia/patches/373307D9 - Dance Central 3.patch.toml.

docs.title: With Xenia emulator
docs.text:
1. Download the prebuilt ZIP from the GitHub release.
  2. Copy xenia/patches/373307D9 - Dance Central 3.patch.toml from the ZIP into your Xenia installation's patches/ folder.
  3. Launch definitive.xex (or default.xex if renamed) through Xenia.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Use this page as a quick contributor checklist. For full repository guidance, see the root CONTRIBUTING.md.

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
1. Create a focused branch for one logical change.
  2. Implement the smallest reasonable fix or feature.
  3. Run validation relevant to your touched areas.
  4. Update docs if behavior or workflow changed.
  5. Open a pull request using .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md.

docs.title: Validation commands
docs.text:
./scripts/build.sh
./tools/build.py src bin --vanilla --debug
./scripts/flatten-gen.sh --dry-run
python3 scripts/prune-identical-src.py --dry-run

Run what applies to your change and report exactly what you executed.

docs.title: Pull request expectations
docs.text:
• State what changed and why.
  • Include validation commands and outcomes.
  • Note limitations, risk, and follow-up work.
  • Keep unrelated refactors out of the same PR.

docs.title: AI-assisted contributions
docs.text: If you use AI tools, follow AGENTS.md to keep edits minimal, reviewable, and safe.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.11+
  • Bash (Linux/macOS) or cmd.exe (Windows)
  • Execute permission for platform tools in tools/<platform>/
  • Source content in src/
  • Base binaries in bin/

Optional for docs work:

  • MkDocs + Material theme (mkdocs, mkdocs-material)

docs.title: First build
docs.text:
From repository root:

./scripts/build.sh

On Windows:

scripts\build.bat

Expected result:

  • A generated build.ninja
  • Intermediate files in obj/
  • Final output in out/

docs.title: Verify tool entrypoint
docs.text:
./tools/build.py src bin --help

If this works, your Python/tooling path is healthy.

docs.title: Optional: run docs locally
docs.text:
cd docs
mkdocs serve

MkDocs prints a local URL. Open it to preview docs changes.

docs.title: Typical next step
docs.text:
Build all major variants:

./tools/build.py src bin --vanilla --debug

docs.title: Repository license
docs.text:
Original code and original project-authored content in this repository are licensed under the MIT License.

See the repository root LICENSE file for full terms.

docs.title: Scope exclusions
docs.text:
The MIT license in this repository does not apply to proprietary game IP.

The following remain owned by their respective rights holders, including Harmonix Music Systems and Epic Games:

  • stock/original game assets,
  • stock/original data files,
  • stock/original binaries and executable components,
  • trademarks, branding, audio, and video,
  • any other third-party proprietary content.

docs.title: Project intent
docs.text:
This is a non-commercial fan preservation initiative.

No copyright infringement is intended.

docs.title: Contributor expectations
docs.text: By contributing, you confirm you have the right to submit your changes and agree your original contributions are licensed under MIT in this repository, unless explicitly stated otherwise.

docs.title: Source and generated paths
docs.text:
• Edit source content under src/
  • Build intermediates are generated in obj/
  • Final build artifacts are emitted into out/

Do not manually edit generated files inside obj/.

docs.title: Recommended iteration loop
docs.text:
1. Change data/assets in src/.
  2. Run build.
  3. Inspect output in out/.
  4. Test in emulator/hardware setup.
  5. Repeat.

docs.title: Managing generated gen folders
docs.text:
When conversion output lands in nested gen directories and you need to flatten paths, use:

./scripts/flatten-gen.sh --dry-run
./scripts/flatten-gen.sh

Use --force to overwrite collisions.

docs.title: Pruning unchanged source files
docs.text:
To remove files from src/ that are byte-identical to orig/:

python3 scripts/prune-identical-src.py --dry-run
python3 scripts/prune-identical-src.py

This keeps your mod source tree focused on real deltas.

docs.title: Safety notes
docs.text:
• Prefer dry-runs before destructive scripts.
  • Keep orig/ untouched as a reference baseline.
  • Commit in small, reviewable chunks.

docs.title: Play the Game
docs.text: Use this page if you just want to install the release and play, without dealing with the build system.

docs.title: What you need
docs.text:
• A copy of the prebuilt ZIP from the latest GitHub release
  • Your game folder, the one that already contains default.xex and gen/
  • Optional: Xenia, if you want to play on PC

docs.title: Install on console or handheld setups
docs.text:
1. Download the prebuilt ZIP from the GitHub release.
  2. Extract it into your game folder.
  3. Launch definitive.xex.

If you want the base game hidden from the dashboard, back up default.xex first and then rename definitive.xex to default.xex.

docs.title: Install for Xenia on PC
docs.text:
1. Download the prebuilt ZIP from the GitHub release.
  2. Copy xenia/patches/373307D9 - Dance Central 3.patch.toml from the ZIP into your Xenia installation's patches/ folder.
  3. Launch definitive.xex in Xenia.

If you renamed definitive.xex to default.xex, launch that file instead.

docs.title: What the ZIP contains
docs.text:
• definitive.xex for the rebuilt game
  • gen/ with the generated patch data
  • xenia/patches/373307D9 - Dance Central 3.patch.toml for Xenia users

docs.title: If something goes wrong
docs.text: If the game does not start, check Troubleshooting.

docs.title: Build script runs but build fails
docs.text:
Check the terminal for the first failing Ninja rule.

Common causes:

  • missing or non-executable binaries under tools/<platform>/
  • invalid source data syntax (for example .dta issues)
  • path mistakes in custom includes

docs.title: tools/build.py does not execute directly on Linux
docs.text:
Verify line endings and executable bit:

file tools/build.py
chmod +x tools/build.py

Then retest:

./tools/build.py src bin --help

docs.title: Xenia launch does not start
docs.text:
• confirm --xenia-path points to a real executable
  • ensure selected XEX exists in out/
  • reduce --xenia-args to isolate bad flags

docs.title: Unexpected missing files in src/
docs.text:
If you ran pruning/flatten scripts, rerun with --dry-run first next time.

Recovery options:

  • restore from git history
  • restore from backup copy
  • re-extract from orig/ baseline

docs.title: Docs preview does not start
docs.text:
Install dependencies in your active Python environment:

pip install mkdocs mkdocs-material

Then run:

cd docs
mkdocs serve

docs.title: What is supported
docs.text:
The build tool can:

  • generate/update a DC3 patch TOML in Xenia patches/
  • launch a selected XEX after build

docs.title: Required flags
docs.text:
• --xenia-path <path> must point to your Xenia executable/AppImage
  • --xenia-run chooses what to run

Run selector values:

  • 0 = disabled
  • 1 = deluxe.xex
  • 2 = vanilla.xex
  • 3 = deluxe_debug.xex
  • 4 = vanilla_debug.xex

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
./tools/build.py src bin \
  --vanilla --debug \
  --xenia-path ./xenia/xenia.AppImage \
  --xenia-patch \
  --xenia-run 1 \
  --xenia-args "--fullscreen"

docs.title: Patch behavior
docs.text:
When --xenia-patch is enabled, the build attempts to maintain patch content and append known module hashes for generated XEX variants.

Patch target location is based on the Xenia executable parent directory:

  • <xenia_root>/patches/373307D9 - Dance Central 3.patch.toml

docs.title: Common pitfalls
docs.text:
• --xenia-run without --xenia-path is invalid.
  • Launch fails if selected XEX was not built/copied to out/.
  • Use absolute paths when testing from different working directories.
